The bioactive constituents of Arrhenatherum elatius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Arrhenatherum elatius is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried flowers and leaves for use in traditional medicine and homeopathy.
